{{Infobox book
| name = Threshold: Stories from Cradle
| slug = cradle-13-threshold
| precedes = [[The Captain (novel)|The Captain]]
| follows = [[Waybound (novel)|Waybound]]
| cover = [[File:Cover-cradle-13-threshold.jpg|250px]]
}}

== Plot summary ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The collection of stories within '[[Threshold]]: Stories from [[Cradle]]' explores various perspectives and events across the multiverse. The narrative begins with [[Reigan Shen]], an ambitious [[Underlord]] of the [[Shen tribe]], who secures victory in the Uncrowned King Tournament{{!}}Uncrowned King tournament by forcibly advancing to Overlord, earning a [[Monarch]]'s treasury and a sorrowful prophecy. In another realm, Eithan{{!}}Eithan Arelius engages in a profound conversation with a [[Forged]] echo of his past self, [[Ozmanthus Arelius]], reflecting on their shared history and the burdens of being the [[Reaper of Worlds]]. Meanwhile, [[Yan Shoumei]], after a devastating loss in a tournament and an attack on her family by the [[Archlord]] [[Anagi]], is rescued and restored by [[Lindon Arelius]], who incinerates her antagonist. 

On a cosmic scale, [[Northstrider]] is assigned to the [[Ghost Division]] after demonstrating rare aptitude for all seven [[Abidan]] disciplines; his attempt to restructure a collapsing world's timeline earns him the respect of the [[Judge]] [[Durandiel]]. In a desperate raid on the [[Tomb of Scrolls]], [[Zeth Baker]] and his [[Worldweavers]] attempt to stop the summoning of the goddess Mul'Tanak, only for the goddess to arrive and execute the very priests who summoned her. On [[Pioneer 602]], [[Mercy]] and [[Akura Fury]] battle the [[Class Three Fiend]] [[Jek’nan]], a conflict that ends with [[Eithan]] personally executing the creature. 

[[Lindon]]'s journey continues as he takes on his first mission as a [[Reaper]] to reclaim the world of [[Threshold]] from Silverlords{{!}}Silverlord occupiers, eventually negotiating his release through the intervention of the [[Titan]] [[Gadrael]]. In a different setting, [[Kelsa]], [[Lindon]]'s sister, enters an arena under the alias '[[Daughter of Dread]],' where she uses [[Lindon]]'s [[pure madra]] to intervene in a fight before being joined by the [[Herald]] [[Balari]] and [[Lindon]]'s [[Fleshripper Senior]] [[construct]]. Political intrigue unfolds in the [[Seishen Kingdom]] as [[Riyusai Meira]] observes the return of [[Prince Daji]], realizing the prince has been hollowed out to serve as a controlled instrument for the [[Akura clan]]. 

Training and humanitarian efforts are also highlighted, as [[Akura Pride]] struggles through the [[Wolf Division]] and witnesses the devastation caused by Ozriel's absence. In [[Sarcoline City]], [[Ziel]] and his team successfully sabotage [[Eternity Incorporated]] by using a massive [[script-circle]] to sever an experimental [[dragon]]'s connection to the [[Iteration]], preventing a catastrophe. The [[Reaper Division]], led by [[Eithan]], engages in a team-building exercise in the Void, using [[Lindon]]'s [[binding]] abilities to [[construct]] a new [[Iteration]] named '[[Windfall]].' Finally, [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] visit [[Greenpeak]], where they find a moment of connection amidst the arrival of many powerful acquaintances. The collection concludes with a series of fictionalized alternative endings for the [[Cradle]] series and an advertisement.}}

== Chapter-by-chapter ==
* '''Chapter 1:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Reigan Shen]], an ambitious [[Underlord]] of the [[Shen tribe]], joins [[House Gray]] to compete in the Uncrowned King Tournament{{!}}Uncrowned King tournament hosted by the [[Monarchs]]. After a grueling series of matches where he defeats formidable opponents like [[Tiberian Arelius]], Reigan Shen{{!}}Reigan forcibly advances to Overlord to secure his victory. Upon winning the tournament, he is granted a [[Monarch]]'s treasury and receives both approval and sorrowful prophecy from the reigning [[Monarchs]].}}
* '''Chapter 2:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|While [[Lindon]] prepares to fight a separate opponent, [[Eithan]] uses the [[Forged]] echo of his past self, [[Ozmanthus Arelius]], to create a private space for conversation. The two versions of the same man discuss their shared history involving the [[Abidan]] and the heavy burden of their past roles as the [[Reaper of Worlds]]. After reflecting on their failures and lessons learned, they share a moment of mutual understanding before [[Eithan]] prepares to continue his journey with [[Lindon]].}}
* '''Chapter 3:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|After losing a tournament match and being left for dead, [[Yan Shoumei]] returns home to find her adoptive family under attack by the [[Flashing Knives]] and her long-time antagonist, the [[Archlord]] [[Anagi]]. Following a brutal battle that results in the destruction of her [[Blood Shadow]], [[Crusher]], [[Yan Shoumei]] desperately contacts [[Lindon Arelius]] for help. [[Lindon]] uses his power to restore her and then incinerates [[Anagi]], leaving [[Yan Shoumei]] alive while the [[Archlord]]'s remains are claimed by spirits.}}
* '''Chapter 4:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|After passing an aptitude test with rare compatibility for all seven [[Abidan]] disciplines, [[Northstrider]] is assigned to the [[Ghost Division]] and taken to a collapsing world. There, he uses his Authority{{!}}authority to attempt a massive restructuring of the world's broken timeline, an act that draws the attention of a powerful [[Judge]] named [[Durandiel]]. Impressed by his creativity and vision, [[Durandiel]] demonstrates the true scale of [[Ghost]] techniques before inviting him to join her division.}}
* '''Chapter 5:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Zeth Baker]] and his team of [[Worldweavers]] launch a desperate, suicidal raid on the [[Tomb of Scrolls]] to stop the [[Tanakite Empire]] from summoning their goddess, Mul'Tanak. During the bloody assault, most of [[Zeth]]'s companions are killed or mortally wounded before the goddess actually manifests. Upon her arrival, Mul'Tanak expresses disdain for the murder-cult that summoned her and uses her power to instantly execute the Tanakite priests, leaving a stunned [[Zeth]] as the sole survivor to interact with her.}}
* '''Chapter 6:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Mercy]] and her half-brother [[Akura Fury]] are dispatched to [[Pioneer 602]] to investigate anomalous [[fate]] projections, only to discover they are being targeted by [[Jek’nan]], a [[Class Three Fiend]] known as the [[Flourishing Disease]]. To protect the local population, [[Mercy]] and [[Fury]] execute a plan to evacuate the workers and intentionally destabilize the world's reality to bait the enemy. After a fierce battle where [[Mercy]] pursues the [[Fiend]] into the Void and wounds him, [[Eithan]] appears to personally execute [[Jek’nan]].}}
* '''Chapter 7:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Lindon]] is assigned his first mission as a [[Reaper]] to reclaim the world of [[Threshold]] from Silverlords{{!}}Silverlord occupiers. After being bound by restrictive oaths from the [[Titan]] [[Gadrael]], [[Lindon]] defeats the local Silverlords{{!}}Silverlord but is subsequently ambushed by a [[Titan]] squad attempting to contain him. The conflict concludes when [[Gadrael]] personally intervenes to negotiate [[Lindon]]'s release, ultimately punishing the [[Titan]] squad for their failure and handing them over to [[Lindon]] for judgment.}}
* '''Chapter 8:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kelsa]], the sister of [[Lindon]], attends a [[Wei clan]] meeting where she observes her elders' political maneuvering before deciding to seek real combat experience by entering an arena under the alias "[[Daughter of Dread]]." After defeating the champion [[Bloody Thorn]], [[Kelsa]] discovers the woman being attacked by a group of [[sacred artists]] and intervenes using [[Lindon]]'s [[pure madra]]. The situation is ultimately resolved when [[Balari]], the [[Herald of the Silent Servants]], arrives to subdue the attackers, followed by the arrival of [[Lindon]]'s [[Fleshripper Senior]] [[construct]].}}
* '''Chapter 9:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|After being informed by a [[Sage]] that [[Prince Daji]] has been returned to the [[Seishen Kingdom]] as a loyal vassal, [[Riyusai Meira]] witnesses [[King Dakata]]'s emotional and suspicious reaction to his son's return. Although [[Daji]] appears physically weakened and speaks in unnervingly perfect, scripted lines of loyalty to the [[Akura clan]], he is welcomed back by a celebratory court. [[Meira]] confronts the transformed prince, realizing that while his personality has been hollowed out to mimic the late Prince Seishen Kiro{{!}}Prince Kiro, he will now serve the kingdom as a controlled instrument of the [[Akura]].}}
* '''Chapter 10:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Akura Pride]] undergoes rigorous training under [[Trainer Mura]] in the [[Wolf Division]], where he struggles to meet the high standards of other recruits and spars with [[Orthos]]. Tensions rise when [[Pride]]'s sister, [[Mercy]], visits, causing friction between the [[Wolf]] and [[Reaper]] Divisions. The chapter concludes with [[Pride]] participating in a humanitarian mission to a devastated world, where he witnesses the aftermath of a [[Fiend]] attack and reflects on the chaos caused by Ozriel's absence.}}
* '''Chapter 11:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Disguised as a local tamer, [[Ziel]] and his companions infiltrate [[Sarcoline City]] to sabotage [[Eternity Incorporated]], a corporation conducting dangerous monster experiments. After rescuing a vengeful survivor named [[Carmine]], [[Ziel]]'s team confronts [[G-42]], an experimental [[dragon]] that has gained a world-threatening connection to the planet's power. Using a massive [[script-circle]] to sever [[G-42]]'s connection to the [[Iteration]], [[Ziel]] successfully subdues the creature and prevents a global catastrophe.}}
* '''Chapter 12:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Suriel]] visits Ozriel at The Grave{{!}}the Grave to discuss the stability of the [[Abidan]] and the potential for the [[Reaper Division]] to succeed where others failed. Following this, [[Eithan]] leads the [[Reaper Division]] into the Void to engage in a team-building exercise: creating a new world from various fragments. Using [[Lindon]]'s ability to bind materials, the group successfully constructs and names their new [[Iteration]] '[[Windfall]].'}}
* '''Chapter 13:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|After emerging from a [[Way-portal]], [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] hike to [[Greenpeak]], [[Yerin]]'s birthplace, where they find the town has grown significantly since her childhood. Their attempt at a quiet visit is interrupted by the arrival of various powerful figures, including [[Lindon]]'s family, [[Charity]], and [[Larian]]. Despite the crowd, [[Yerin]] finds comfort in the bustling town, and she and [[Lindon]] share a moment of connection as they prepare to join the group for dinner.}}
* '''Chapter 14:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|This chapter presents a series of fictionalized alternative endings for each volume of the [[Cradle]] series, ranging from comedic scenarios to violent resolutions. The reports detail various outcomes for characters like [[Lindon]], [[Yerin]], and [[Eithan]], often involving unexpected uses of weaponry or sudden shifts in power dynamics. These vignettes serve as a collection of "what if" scenarios rather than the actual plot of the novel.}}
* '''Chapter 15:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The provided text does not contain any narrative content from a fantasy novel. It consists entirely of an advertisement for Will Wight's mailing list.}}

== Characters ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Lindon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Dross]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Reigan Shen]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Yan Shoumei]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Eithan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Orthos]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Ziel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Mercy]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Ozriel (Cradle)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Gadrael]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Little Blue]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kelsa]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Yerin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Northstrider]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Anagi]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Fury]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Bloody Thorn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Suriel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Mul’Tanak]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Daji]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Tiberian Arelius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kahn Mala]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Jek'nan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Cirian]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[G-42]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Crusher]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Presence]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Ozmanthus Arelius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Yarech]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Carmine]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Emriss Silentborn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Akura Pride]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Seshethkunaaz]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Akura Fury]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Lindon Arelius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Shadeseeker]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Dellei Twinfire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Hall]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Trainer Mura]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kelthryss]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Makiel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Silent King]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Heart Sage]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Meira]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Durandiel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Valketh]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Mist Sage]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Brani]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Vall]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|white-feathered creature}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Titan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Weeping Dragon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Wandering Titan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Malice]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Pariana]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[King Dakata]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Pride]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Seishen Daji]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Uncle Fury]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Mad King]]}}

== Locations & groups ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Abidan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Silverlords]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Reaper Division]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Eternity Incorporated]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Court of Seven (Cradle)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Sanctum]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Cradle]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[House Gray]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Harness]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Wolf Division]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Redmoon Hall]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[House Arelius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Sarcoline City]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Vroshir]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Seishen Kingdom]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Wolves]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Monarchs]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[The Grave]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Ghost Division]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Grave]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Pioneer 602]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Wei clan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Blackflame Empire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Phoenix Division]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Tomb of Scrolls]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Eternity]]}}

== Abilities & sacred arts ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Shield Icon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[vital aura]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Ruler technique]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Hollow Domain]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Joy Icon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Enforcer technique]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[soulfire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Burning Cloak]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Strength Icon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Fox Mirror]]}}

== Artifacts & constructs ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Midnight Rain]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|oracle codex}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Tempest Jar]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Shattersight]]}}

== Events & concepts ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Reaper]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Way]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Iteration]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Threshold]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Blood Shadow]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Void Icon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Phoenix]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Fate]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Uncrowned King Tournament]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[The Void (Cradle)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Dreadgods]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Monarch]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Fiend]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Flourishing Disease]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Unsouled]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Path of the White Fox]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Dreadgod]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[The Way]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Blackflame]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Truegold]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Bleeding Phoenix]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Dread War]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[OTEP]]}}

== Sources ==
* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cradle

{{Willverse books nav}}

[[Category:Books]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
